entries.definition:
كتاب الواو

وأب - وأد

(وأد) الواو والهمزة والدال: كلمةٌ تدلُّ على إثقال شيءٍ بشيء. يقال للإبل إذا مَشَت بثَقَلِها: لها وئيدٌ. قال:

* ما للجمالِ مشيَُِهَا وَئيدَا *

أي مشياً بِثِقَل. والموءودة من هذا، لأنَّها تُدفَن حيّة، فهي تُثْقَل بالتُّراب الذي يعلوها. وَأدَها يَئِدُها وَأْداً. ومن ذلك قوله :

* وأحْيَا الوَئيدَ فلم يُوأدِ *

وأر - وأص - وأق - وأل

who bow down and prostrate themselves; who order what is good
and forbid what is wrong; (39:71) حَتَّى إِذَا جَاءُوهَا فُتِحَتْ أَبْوَابُهَا وَقَالَ لَهُمْ
خَزَنَتُهَا till, when they come to it, its gates are opened, and its
keepers say to them; (66:5) عَسَى رَبُّهُ إِنْ طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَنْ يُبْدِلَهُ أَزْوَاجًا خَيْرًا مِنْكُنَّ
ثَيِّبَاتٍ وَأَبْكَارًا his Lord may well replace you with better wives [if the
Prophet decides to divorce any of you]:
wives who are devoted to God, true believers, devout, who turn to
Him in repentance and worship Him, given to fasting, whether
previously married or virgins. Other commentators, however,
have concluded that these usages of wa are either conjunctive or
circumstantial, leading them to the conclusion that the wa of eight
occurs only once in the Qur’an, in verse (18:22), as cited above.

ثَيِّبَاتٍ وَأَبْكَارًا his Lord may well replace you with better wives [if the

occurs only once in the Qur’an, in verse (18:22), as cited above.

entries.definition:
w–ʾ–d loud thudding sound, sound of heavy steps on the
ground; to be swallowed by the earth; to bury alive, particularly a
newborn girl; to be deliberate, solemnity. Of this root, only مَوْءُودَة
mawʾūdatun, occurs once in the Qur’an.

مَوْءُودَة mawʾūdatun [pass. part.] a female infant buried alive.
The live burial of baby girls was a fairly common practice in
pre-Islamic Arabia, particularly amongst the Bedouin poor (81:8–
9) وَإِذَا الْمَوْءُودَةُ سُئِلَتْ بِأَيِّ ذَنْبٍ قُتِلَتْ [on the day] when the baby girl
buried alive is asked for what crime she was killed.
